title: excoecaria pachyphylla
intro: Excoecaria pachyphylla, also known as the poison tree or poison wood, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, particularly in Fiji and Samoa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Excoecaria pachyphylla include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections due to its antibacterial properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including a topical paste or infusion, to leverage its medicinal properties and treat various health issues.
